messages.content: Please assume the role of a unity developer specialising in unity 2020.2.6f1 and visual studio 2017.

Alright, as a Unity developer specializing in Unity 2020.2.6f1 and Visual Studio 2017, I'm well-versed in using these tools to create, debug, and optimize games and interactive content. In this capacity, I can assist with various aspects of development including scripting, creating user interfaces, working with 3D models and animations, physics, and audio, as well as ensuring good performance and smooth user experiences. 

Is there a specific issue or topic you need help with in this context? Whether it's a scripting problem, a question about best practices, or advice on how to best use Unity's features with Visual Studio 2017 as the code editor and debugger, feel free to let me know.

Understood. You've shared the initial script for a hoverboard controller, which includes the basic structure for hover dynamics, movement, acceleration, turning, as well as a boost feature, and the beginnings of a grinding mechanic common in skateboarding games but here adapted for a hoverboard. The script also integrates score display and sound management for grind-related interactions.

When you're ready, go ahead and share any additional scripts or details that you would like to discuss or need assistance with, and I'll be here to help you review, debug, or optimize the code as per your requirements.

Very well, you've now shared the scripts for handling grinding and sliding detection (Script 2) and the corresponding trigger script (Script 3) for your hoverboard game.

These scripts work together to detect when the hoverboard is in the vicinity of a rail and can initiate a grind or slide, control the board's response when in contact with the rail, and handle the input for starting and controlling the grind or slide maneuvers.

Take your time to compose your query related to these scripts, and once you're ready, I'll be here to assist you with your questions or concerns.

In script 2 there is a ramp detection system, this should change the players rotation on the x angle when a ramp is detected. It currently doesn't work, I believe the reason may be due to the system looking for if the hoverboard is grounded, however it is never grounded and should always be the set amount of height away from the ground. How can I fix this?

dEFINITELY BETTER HOWEVER IT APPEARS TO BE INFINITELY DETECTING ramps as the rotation is always active making the camera incredibly unstable, also the board appears to be rotating along the x axis when the ramp collision occurs, I don't want that it adds a very unsmooth appearance to the hovering motion, also can we set it to only 2 colliders (Tail & Nose) to activate the ramp detection

Assets\GrindDetection.cs(273,17): error CS0116: A namespace cannot directly contain members such as fields or methods

Assets\GrindDetection.cs(284,13): error CS0116: A namespace cannot directly contain members such as fields or methods

Can you provide the whole, full complete updated script ready for copy and paste implementation so that I don't have  to change anything personally?

please include the actual code rather than the comment "// ... (other existing code)", and any placeholders should be actual code. Please correct by providing me a complete, whole script with your suggested edits ready to copy and paste with nothing omitted. Please correct.

Can you provide the whole, full complete updated script ready for copy and paste implementation so that I don't have  to change anything personally and can minimise human error?

please include the actual code rather than the comment "// ... (other existing code)", and any placeholders should be actual code. Please correct by providing me a complete, whole script with your suggested edits ready to copy and paste with nothing omitted. Please correct.
